- [Virginia Cooperative Extension - Home Vegetable Gardening in Virginia (Table 3)](https://www.pubs.ext.vt.edu/426/426-331/426-331.html)

title: Red Beet

frontmatter.Start Method: Direct sow or cell trays; transplant when small.

html:
Red Beet

Quick Use Guide

  • Seed depth: 1/2 in
  • Spacing: rows 12-18 in, plants 2-3 in
  • Feeding level: Light
  • Companion group: Root

Planting Calendar (Zone 7B)

  • Start window: Mar-Apr and Jul-Aug
  • Indoor start method: Direct sow or cell trays; transplant when small.
  • Harvest window: May-Jun and Sep-Nov

Seed Starting and Bloom Timing

  • Days to germination: 5-10 days
  • Bloom from seed: 50-70 days
  • Bloom speed tier: Fast
  • Indoor bloom viability: Yes

Companion Plants

  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Marigold]]
  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Nasturtium]]
  • [[Master-Indexes/Companion Matrix]]

Varieties

  • Detroit Dark Red: classic table beet
  • Red Ace: uniform sweet roots
  • Bull's Blood: deep red roots and leaves

Crop Notes

Useful for root production and for managed bolting to support early pollinators.

Bloom Timing and Pollinator Notes

  • Start method: Direct sow or cell trays; transplant when small.
  • Days to germination: 5-10 days
  • Bloom from seed: 50-70 days
  • Pollinator value: Moderate

Radish

Quick Use Guide

  • Seed depth: 1/4-1/2 in
  • Spacing: rows 12-24 in, plants 1-2 in
  • Feeding level: Light
  • Rotation note: Fast turnover crop for succession gaps

Planting Calendar (Zone 7B)

  • Spring sow/plant window: Mar 1-Apr 15
  • Fall sow/plant window: Aug 15-Sep 15
  • Outdoor transplant window: Direct sow only
  • Indoor start lead time: Direct sow preferred
  • Frost baseline used: last spring frost Apr 5-Apr 15; first fall frost Oct 25-Nov 5.

Seed Depth and Spacing

  • Seed/planting depth: 1/4-1/2 in
  • Row spacing: 12-24 in
  • In-row spacing: 1-2 in
  • Timing windows verified against Virginia Cooperative Extension (Table 3) and Clemson Extension planting chart.

Transplanting Time and Procedure

  • Direct sow/planting is the standard method for this crop.
  • Prepare a fine seedbed and keep topsoil evenly moist through germination.
  • Thin seedlings early to final spacing to reduce disease pressure.

Companion Plants

  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Onion]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Lettuce]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Pea]]
  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Chives]]

Avoid Planting Near

  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Dill]] (especially with carrots when mature)
  • Compacted soil and fresh manure

Varieties

  • Cherry Belle: quick spring globe radish
  • French Breakfast: elongated mild roots
  • Daikon: long storage and cover-crop root

Crop Notes

Sow in short intervals to prevent glut and pithiness.

title: Pumpkin

frontmatter.Start Method: See planting calendar and crop notes for direct sow vs transplant guidance.
frontmatter.Tags: pumpkin, zone-7b, vegetable, bloom-fast, pollinator-moderate, pollinator-high

html:
Pumpkin

Quick Use Guide

  • Seed depth: 1 in
  • Spacing: rows 60-96 in, plants 24-60 in
  • Feeding level: Heavy
  • Rotation note: Keep out of same bed as cucumbers/squash for 2-3 years

Planting Calendar (Zone 7B)

  • Spring sow/plant window: Jun 15-Jul 1
  • Outdoor transplant window: Direct sow preferred
  • Indoor start lead time: Optional 2-week start in compostable pots
  • Frost baseline used: last spring frost Apr 5-Apr 15; first fall frost Oct 25-Nov 5.

Seed Depth and Spacing

  • Seed/planting depth: 1 in
  • Row spacing: 60-96 in
  • In-row spacing: 24-60 in
  • Timing windows verified against Virginia Cooperative Extension (Table 3) and Clemson Extension planting chart.

Transplanting Time and Procedure

  • Direct sow is effective once soil is warm enough.
  • Optional transplanting can speed harvest by 1-2 weeks.
  • Harden off starts for 7-10 days and avoid root disturbance.
  • Water in deeply after planting and keep moisture steady until new growth.

Companion Plants

  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Bean Bush]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Corn Sweet]]
  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Dill]]
  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Nasturtium]]

Avoid Planting Near

  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Potato]]
  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Fennel]]

Varieties

  • Small Sugar: dense sweet pie flesh
  • Howden: classic jack-o-lantern type
  • Jack Be Little: mini ornamental edible

Crop Notes

Match maturity days to desired October harvest date.

Verified References

  • Virginia Cooperative Extension - Home Vegetable Gardening in Virginia (Table 3)
  • Clemson Cooperative Extension - Planning a Garden (Table 2)
  • WVU Extension - Companion Planting in the Garden
  • University of Maryland Extension - Planting Transplants in Your Garden

Bloom Timing and Pollinator Notes

  • Start method: See planting calendar and crop notes for direct sow vs transplant guidance.
  • Days to germination: 5-14 days
  • Bloom from seed: 50-90 days
  • Pollinator value: Moderate

title: Pepper

frontmatter.Start Method: See planting calendar and crop notes for direct sow vs transplant guidance.

html:
Pepper

Quick Use Guide

  • Seed depth: 1/4 in seed; set transplants about 3 in deep
  • Spacing: rows 24-36 in, plants 15-18 in
  • Feeding level: Heavy
  • Rotation note: Do not follow tomato, potato, or eggplant

Planting Calendar (Zone 7B)

  • Spring sow/plant window: May 1-Jun 15
  • Outdoor transplant window: May 1-Jun 15
  • Indoor start lead time: Start 6-8 weeks before transplanting
  • Frost baseline used: last spring frost Apr 5-Apr 15; first fall frost Oct 25-Nov 5.

Seed Depth and Spacing

  • Seed/planting depth: 1/4 in seed; set transplants about 3 in deep
  • Row spacing: 24-36 in
  • In-row spacing: 15-18 in
  • Timing windows verified against Virginia Cooperative Extension (Table 3) and Clemson Extension planting chart.

Transplanting Time and Procedure

  • Best method: transplant starts/slips/crowns in the window above.
  • Harden off starts for 7-10 days before planting outdoors.
  • Transplant in late afternoon or on cloudy days to reduce shock.
  • Water in thoroughly and mulch after soil has warmed.

Companion Plants

  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Basil]]
  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Marigold]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Onion]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Garlic]]

Avoid Planting Near

  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Fennel]]
  • Other disease-prone Solanaceae in the same bed year after year

Varieties

  • California Wonder: blocky sweet bell
  • Jalapeno: medium-heat hot pepper
  • Poblano: mild heat roasting pepper

Crop Notes

Use row cover early to speed establishment and block pests.

title: Pea

frontmatter.Start Method: See planting calendar and crop notes for direct sow vs transplant guidance.

html:
Pea

Quick Use Guide

  • Seed depth: 1-1.5 in
  • Spacing: rows 18-24 in, plants 2-3 in
  • Feeding level: Nitrogen Fixer
  • Rotation note: Plant before heavy summer feeders

Planting Calendar (Zone 7B)

  • Spring sow/plant window: Mar 1-Apr 15
  • Fall sow/plant window: Jul 15-Aug 1
  • Outdoor transplant window: Direct sow preferred
  • Indoor start lead time: Direct sow in cool soil
  • Frost baseline used: last spring frost Apr 5-Apr 15; first fall frost Oct 25-Nov 5.

Seed Depth and Spacing

  • Seed/planting depth: 1-1.5 in
  • Row spacing: 18-24 in
  • In-row spacing: 2-3 in
  • Timing windows verified against Virginia Cooperative Extension (Table 3) and Clemson Extension planting chart.

Transplanting Time and Procedure

  • Direct sow/planting is the standard method for this crop.
  • Prepare a fine seedbed and keep topsoil evenly moist through germination.
  • Thin seedlings early to final spacing to reduce disease pressure.

Companion Plants

  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Corn Sweet]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Cucumber]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Radish]]
  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Marigold]]

Avoid Planting Near

  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Onion]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Garlic]]

Varieties

  • Sugar Snap: edible pod snap pea
  • Green Arrow: shelling pea with long picking window
  • Little Marvel: compact shelling pea

Crop Notes

Trellis even semi-dwarf types for cleaner harvest.

Verified References

  • Virginia Cooperative Extension - Home Vegetable Gardening in Virginia (Table 3)
  • Clemson Cooperative Extension - Planning a Garden (Table 2)
  • WVU Extension - Companion Planting in the Garden
  • University of Maryland Extension - Planting Transplants in Your Garden

  • Days to germination: 5-14 days
  • Bloom from seed: 50-90 days
  • Pollinator value: Moderate

frontmatter.Start Method: See planting calendar and crop notes for direct sow vs transplant guidance.

html:
Parsnip

Quick Use Guide

  • Seed depth: 1/2 in
  • Spacing: rows 18-24 in, plants 3-6 in
  • Feeding level: Light
  • Rotation note: Follow heavy feeders or corn

Planting Calendar (Zone 7B)

  • Spring sow/plant window: Feb 15-Apr 30
  • Outdoor transplant window: Direct sow only
  • Indoor start lead time: Direct sow fresh seed
  • Frost baseline used: last spring frost Apr 5-Apr 15; first fall frost Oct 25-Nov 5.

Seed Depth and Spacing

  • Seed/planting depth: 1/2 in
  • Row spacing: 18-24 in
  • In-row spacing: 3-6 in
  • Timing windows verified against Virginia Cooperative Extension (Table 3) and Clemson Extension planting chart.

Transplanting Time and Procedure

  • Direct sow/planting is the standard method for this crop.
  • Prepare a fine seedbed and keep topsoil evenly moist through germination.
  • Thin seedlings early to final spacing to reduce disease pressure.

Companion Plants

  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Onion]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Lettuce]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Pea]]
  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Chives]]

Avoid Planting Near

  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Dill]] (especially with carrots when mature)

title: Parsley

frontmatter.Start Method: See planting calendar and crop notes for direct sow vs transplant guidance.

html:
Parsley

Quick Use Guide

  • Seed depth: 1/4-1/2 in
  • Spacing: rows 12-24 in, plants 3-6 in
  • Feeding level: Light
  • Rotation note: Light feeder useful as bed-edge crop

Planting Calendar (Zone 7B)

  • Spring sow/plant window: Mar 15-Apr 15
  • Fall sow/plant window: Jul 15-Aug 1
  • Outdoor transplant window: Mar-Apr and Aug
  • Indoor start lead time: Start 6-8 weeks before transplanting
  • Frost baseline used: last spring frost Apr 5-Apr 15; first fall frost Oct 25-Nov 5.

Seed Depth and Spacing

  • Seed/planting depth: 1/4-1/2 in
  • Row spacing: 12-24 in
  • In-row spacing: 3-6 in
  • Timing windows verified against Virginia Cooperative Extension (Table 3) and Clemson Extension planting chart.

Transplanting Time and Procedure

  • Direct sow is effective once soil is warm enough.
  • Optional transplanting can speed harvest by 1-2 weeks.
  • Harden off starts for 7-10 days and avoid root disturbance.
  • Water in deeply after planting and keep moisture steady until new growth.

Companion Plants

  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Carrot]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Radish]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Onion]]
  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Chives]]

Avoid Planting Near

  • Crowded blocks with poor airflow
  • Hot, dry, reflected-heat beds

Varieties

  • Flat-leaf Italian: strong culinary flavor
  • Curled: decorative dense foliage
  • Hamburg: grown for edible root and tops

Crop Notes

Soak seed overnight to speed germination.

Verified References

  • Virginia Cooperative Extension - Home Vegetable Gardening in Virginia (Table 3)
  • Clemson Cooperative Extension - Planning a Garden (Table 2)
  • WVU Extension - Companion Planting in the Garden
  • University of Maryland Extension - Planting Transplants in Your Garden

Bloom Timing and Pollinator Notes

  • Start method: See planting calendar and crop notes for direct sow vs transplant guidance.
  • Days to germination: 5-14 days
  • Bloom from seed: 50-90 days
  • Pollinator value: Moderate

  • Row spacing: 12-24 in

## Crop Notes

title: Mustard Greens

frontmatter.Start Method: See planting calendar and crop notes for direct sow vs transplant guidance.

html:
Mustard Greens

Quick Use Guide

  • Seed depth: 1/4-1/2 in
  • Spacing: rows 12-24 in, plants 2-4 in
  • Feeding level: Light
  • Rotation note: Fast crop that fits between heavy feeders

Planting Calendar (Zone 7B)

  • Spring sow/plant window: Mar 1-Apr 15
  • Fall sow/plant window: Aug 1-Sep 15
  • Outdoor transplant window: Direct sow preferred
  • Indoor start lead time: Direct sow preferred
  • Frost baseline used: last spring frost Apr 5-Apr 15; first fall frost Oct 25-Nov 5.

Seed Depth and Spacing

  • Seed/planting depth: 1/4-1/2 in
  • Row spacing: 12-24 in
  • In-row spacing: 2-4 in
  • Timing windows verified against Virginia Cooperative Extension (Table 3) and Clemson Extension planting chart.

Transplanting Time and Procedure

  • Direct sow/planting is the standard method for this crop.
  • Prepare a fine seedbed and keep topsoil evenly moist through germination.
  • Thin seedlings early to final spacing to reduce disease pressure.

Companion Plants

  • Mizuna: mild fringed Asian mustard

Successive sow every 2 weeks for steady harvests.

title: Luffa

frontmatter.Start Method: Start indoors 4-6 weeks before transplanting.

html:
Luffa

Quick Use Guide

  • Seed depth: 1 in
  • Spacing: rows 60-72 in, plants 18-24 in
  • Feeding level: Heavy
  • Companion group: Cucurbit

Planting Calendar (Zone 7B)

  • Start window: Apr-May (indoors start in Mar-Apr)
  • Indoor start method: Start indoors 4-6 weeks before transplanting.
  • Harvest window: Aug-Oct

Seed Starting and Bloom Timing

  • Days to germination: 7-14 days
  • Bloom from seed: 55-75 days
  • Bloom speed tier: Fast
  • Indoor bloom viability: Limited

Companion Plants

  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Marigold]]
  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Nasturtium]]
  • [[Master-Indexes/Companion Matrix]]

Varieties

  • Smooth Luffa: long, straight sponge gourds
  • Angled Luffa: ridged fruit, often earlier

Crop Notes

Use trellising for straight fruit and clean sponge quality.

Bloom Timing and Pollinator Notes

  • Start method: Start indoors 4-6 weeks before transplanting.
  • Days to germination: 7-14 days
  • Bloom from seed: 55-75 days
  • Pollinator value: High

title: Lettuce

frontmatter.Start Method: See planting calendar and crop notes for direct sow vs transplant guidance.

html:
Lettuce

Quick Use Guide

  • Seed depth: 1/8 in
  • Spacing: rows 12-18 in, plants 6-12 in
  • Feeding level: Light
  • Rotation note: Excellent quick crop after heavy feeders

Planting Calendar (Zone 7B)

  • Spring sow/plant window: Mar 1-Apr 15
  • Fall sow/plant window: Aug 1-Aug 31
  • Outdoor transplant window: Mar-Apr and Aug-Sep
  • Indoor start lead time: Start 4-6 weeks before transplanting
  • Frost baseline used: last spring frost Apr 5-Apr 15; first fall frost Oct 25-Nov 5.

Seed Depth and Spacing

  • Seed/planting depth: 1/8 in
  • Row spacing: 12-18 in
  • In-row spacing: 6-12 in
  • Timing windows verified against Virginia Cooperative Extension (Table 3) and Clemson Extension planting chart.

Transplanting Time and Procedure

  • Direct sow is effective once soil is warm enough.
  • Optional transplanting can speed harvest by 1-2 weeks.
  • Harden off starts for 7-10 days and avoid root disturbance.
  • Water in deeply after planting and keep moisture steady until new growth.

Companion Plants

  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Carrot]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Radish]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Onion]]
  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Chives]]

Avoid Planting Near

  • Crowded blocks with poor airflow
  • Hot, dry, reflected-heat beds

Varieties

  • Butterhead: soft loose heads
  • Romaine: upright crunchy leaves
  • Looseleaf: cut-and-come-again mix

Crop Notes

Provide afternoon shade for late spring plantings.

Verified References

  • Virginia Cooperative Extension - Home Vegetable Gardening in Virginia (Table 3)
  • Clemson Cooperative Extension - Planning a Garden (Table 2)
  • WVU Extension - Companion Planting in the Garden
  • University of Maryland Extension - Planting Transplants in Your Garden

Bloom Timing and Pollinator Notes

  • Start method: See planting calendar and crop notes for direct sow vs transplant guidance.
  • Days to germination: 5-14 days
  • Bloom from seed: 50-90 days
  • Pollinator value: Moderate

title: Leek

frontmatter.Start Method: See planting calendar and crop notes for direct sow vs transplant guidance.

html:
Leek

Quick Use Guide

  • Seed depth: 1/4 in seed; set transplants about 3 in deep
  • Spacing: rows 24 in, plants 3-4 in
  • Feeding level: Medium
  • Rotation note: Moderate feeder; good before root crops

Planting Calendar (Zone 7B)

  • Spring sow/plant window: Jan 15-Feb 15 (start plants)
  • Fall sow/plant window: Jul 1-Aug 15 (start for fall)
  • Outdoor transplant window: Mar-Apr and Aug-Sep
  • Indoor start lead time: Start 8-10 weeks before transplanting
  • Frost baseline used: last spring frost Apr 5-Apr 15; first fall frost Oct 25-Nov 5.

Seed Depth and Spacing

  • Seed/planting depth: 1/4 in seed; set transplants about 3 in deep
  • Row spacing: 24 in
  • In-row spacing: 3-4 in
  • Timing windows verified against Virginia Cooperative Extension (Table 3) and Clemson Extension planting chart.

Transplanting Time and Procedure

  • Best method: transplant starts/slips/crowns in the window above.
  • Harden off starts for 7-10 days before planting outdoors.
  • Transplant in late afternoon or on cloudy days to reduce shock.
  • Water in thoroughly and mulch after soil has warmed.

Companion Plants

  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Carrot]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Beet]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Lettuce]]
  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Chamomile]]

Avoid Planting Near

  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Bean Bush]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Pea]]

Varieties

  • King Richard: fast maturing summer leek
  • American Flag: classic full-size leek
  • Lancelot: long white shank hybrid

Crop Notes

Hill soil around stems to blanch shanks.

Verified References

  • Virginia Cooperative Extension - Home Vegetable Gardening in Virginia (Table 3)
  • Clemson Cooperative Extension - Planning a Garden (Table 2)
  • WVU Extension - Companion Planting in the Garden
  • University of Maryland Extension - Planting Transplants in Your Garden

Bloom Timing and Pollinator Notes

  • Start method: See planting calendar and crop notes for direct sow vs transplant guidance.
  • Days to germination: 5-14 days
  • Bloom from seed: 50-90 days
  • Pollinator value: Moderate

title: Garlic

frontmatter.Start Method: See planting calendar and crop notes for direct sow vs transplant guidance.

html:
Garlic

Quick Use Guide

  • Seed depth: 1-2 in (cloves)
  • Spacing: rows 6-12 in, plants 3-6 in
  • Feeding level: Medium
  • Rotation note: Good disease-break crop between heavy feeders

Planting Calendar (Zone 7B)

  • Fall sow/plant window: Sep 15-Nov 15
  • Outdoor transplant window: Plant cloves in fall
  • Indoor start lead time: No indoor start needed
  • Frost baseline used: last spring frost Apr 5-Apr 15; first fall frost Oct 25-Nov 5.

Seed Depth and Spacing

  • Seed/planting depth: 1-2 in (cloves)
  • Row spacing: 6-12 in
  • In-row spacing: 3-6 in
  • Timing windows verified against Virginia Cooperative Extension (Table 3) and Clemson Extension planting chart.

Transplanting Time and Procedure

  • Direct sow/planting is the standard method for this crop.
  • Prepare a fine seedbed and keep topsoil evenly moist through germination.
  • Thin seedlings early to final spacing to reduce disease pressure.

Companion Plants

  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Carrot]]

Varieties

  • Music: hardy hardneck with large cloves
  • Inchelium Red: productive softneck keeper
  • Chesnok Red: flavorful purple-striped hardneck

Crop Notes

Mulch heavily after planting for winter protection.

  • Virginia Cooperative Extension - Home Vegetable Gardening in Virginia (Table 3)

title: Eggplant

frontmatter.Start Method: See planting calendar and crop notes for direct sow vs transplant guidance.

html:
Eggplant

Quick Use Guide

  • Seed depth: 1/4 in seed; set transplants about 3 in deep
  • Spacing: rows 24-36 in, plants 24-36 in
  • Feeding level: Heavy
  • Rotation note: Do not follow tomato/pepper/potato

Planting Calendar (Zone 7B)

  • Spring sow/plant window: May 1-Jun 15
  • Outdoor transplant window: May 1-Jun 15
  • Indoor start lead time: Start 6-8 weeks before transplanting
  • Frost baseline used: last spring frost Apr 5-Apr 15; first fall frost Oct 25-Nov 5.

Seed Depth and Spacing

  • Seed/planting depth: 1/4 in seed; set transplants about 3 in deep
  • Row spacing: 24-36 in
  • In-row spacing: 24-36 in
  • Timing windows verified against Virginia Cooperative Extension (Table 3) and Clemson Extension planting chart.

Transplanting Time and Procedure

  • Best method: transplant starts/slips/crowns in the window above.
  • Harden off starts for 7-10 days before planting outdoors.
  • Transplant in late afternoon or on cloudy days to reduce shock.
  • Water in thoroughly and mulch after soil has warmed.

Companion Plants

  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Basil]]
  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Marigold]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Onion]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Garlic]]

Avoid Planting Near

  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Fennel]]
  • Other disease-prone Solanaceae in the same bed year after year

Varieties

  • Black Beauty: classic globe eggplant
  • Ichiban: slender Asian type
  • Fairy Tale: small striped fruits

Crop Notes

Wait for warm nights before setting plants out.

Verified References

  • Virginia Cooperative Extension - Home Vegetable Gardening in Virginia (Table 3)
  • Clemson Cooperative Extension - Planning a Garden (Table 2)
  • WVU Extension - Companion Planting in the Garden
  • University of Maryland Extension - Planting Transplants in Your Garden

Bloom Timing and Pollinator Notes

  • Start method: See planting calendar and crop notes for direct sow vs transplant guidance.
  • Days to germination: 5-14 days
  • Bloom from seed: 50-90 days
  • Pollinator value: Moderate

Daikon Radish

Quick Use Guide

  • Seed depth: 1/2 in
  • Spacing: rows 12-18 in, plants 3-4 in
  • Feeding level: Light
  • Companion group: Root

Planting Calendar (Zone 7B)

  • Start window: Mar-Apr and Aug-Sep

title: Cucumber

frontmatter.Start Method: See planting calendar and crop notes for direct sow vs transplant guidance.
frontmatter.Tags: cucumber, zone-7b, vegetable, bloom-fast, pollinator-moderate, pollinator-high

html:
Cucumber

Quick Use Guide

  • Seed depth: 1 in
  • Spacing: rows 60-72 in, plants 12-36 in
  • Feeding level: Heavy
  • Rotation note: Avoid repeated cucurbit planting in same bed

Planting Calendar (Zone 7B)

  • Spring sow/plant window: Apr 25-Jun 15
  • Fall sow/plant window: Jul 1-Jul 15
  • Outdoor transplant window: Apr 25-May 20 (optional)
  • Indoor start lead time: Start 2-3 weeks before transplanting
  • Frost baseline used: last spring frost Apr 5-Apr 15; first fall frost Oct 25-Nov 5.

Seed Depth and Spacing

  • Seed/planting depth: 1 in
  • Row spacing: 60-72 in
  • In-row spacing: 12-36 in
  • Timing windows verified against Virginia Cooperative Extension (Table 3) and Clemson Extension planting chart.

Transplanting Time and Procedure

  • Direct sow is effective once soil is warm enough.
  • Optional transplanting can speed harvest by 1-2 weeks.
  • Harden off starts for 7-10 days and avoid root disturbance.
  • Water in deeply after planting and keep moisture steady until new growth.

Companion Plants

  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Bean Bush]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Corn Sweet]]
  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Dill]]
  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Nasturtium]]

Avoid Planting Near

  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Potato]]
  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Fennel]]

Varieties

  • Marketmore 76: slicer with good disease resistance
  • Boston Pickling: compact pickling type
  • Diva: smooth burpless fruits

Crop Notes

Trellising improves airflow and straight fruit.

Verified References

  • Virginia Cooperative Extension - Home Vegetable Gardening in Virginia (Table 3)
  • Clemson Cooperative Extension - Planning a Garden (Table 2)
  • WVU Extension - Companion Planting in the Garden
  • University of Maryland Extension - Planting Transplants in Your Garden

Bloom Timing and Pollinator Notes

  • Start method: See planting calendar and crop notes for direct sow vs transplant guidance.
  • Days to germination: 5-14 days
  • Bloom from seed: 50-90 days
  • Pollinator value: Moderate

title: Corn Sweet

frontmatter.Start Method: See planting calendar and crop notes for direct sow vs transplant guidance.

html:
Corn Sweet

Quick Use Guide

  • Seed depth: 1 in
  • Spacing: rows 30-36 in, plants 8-12 in
  • Feeding level: Heavy
  • Rotation note: Heavy feeder; follow with peas/beans

Planting Calendar (Zone 7B)

  • Spring sow/plant window: Apr 15-Jun 10
  • Fall sow/plant window: Jul 1-Jul 15
  • Outdoor transplant window: Direct sow in blocks
  • Indoor start lead time: Direct sow only
  • Frost baseline used: last spring frost Apr 5-Apr 15; first fall frost Oct 25-Nov 5.

Seed Depth and Spacing

  • Seed/planting depth: 1 in
  • Row spacing: 30-36 in
  • In-row spacing: 8-12 in
  • Timing windows verified against Virginia Cooperative Extension (Table 3) and Clemson Extension planting chart.

Transplanting Time and Procedure

  • Direct sow/planting is the standard method for this crop.
  • Prepare a fine seedbed and keep topsoil evenly moist through germination.
  • Thin seedlings early to final spacing to reduce disease pressure.

  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Bean Pole]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Pumpkin]]

title: Celery

frontmatter.Start Method: See planting calendar and crop notes for direct sow vs transplant guidance.

html:
Celery

Quick Use Guide

  • Seed depth: 1/4 in
  • Spacing: rows 24 in, plants 6-8 in
  • Feeding level: Heavy
  • Rotation note: Follow with legumes to rebuild fertility

Planting Calendar (Zone 7B)

  • Spring sow/plant window: Jan 15-Feb 15 (start transplants)
  • Fall sow/plant window: Jul 1-Aug 15 (fall crop starts)
  • Outdoor transplant window: Mar-Apr and Aug-Sep after hardening
  • Indoor start lead time: Start 8-10 weeks before transplanting
  • Frost baseline used: last spring frost Apr 5-Apr 15; first fall frost Oct 25-Nov 5.

Seed Depth and Spacing

  • Seed/planting depth: 1/4 in
  • Row spacing: 24 in
  • In-row spacing: 6-8 in
  • Timing windows verified against Virginia Cooperative Extension (Table 3) and Clemson Extension planting chart.

Transplanting Time and Procedure

  • Best method: transplant starts/slips/crowns in the window above.
  • Harden off starts for 7-10 days before planting outdoors.
  • Transplant in late afternoon or on cloudy days to reduce shock.
  • Water in thoroughly and mulch after soil has warmed.

Companion Plants

  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Carrot]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Radish]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Onion]]
  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Chives]]

Avoid Planting Near

  • Crowded blocks with poor airflow
  • Hot, dry, reflected-heat beds

Varieties

  • Tall Utah: common home-garden celery
  • Tango: vigorous green stalk type
  • Golden Self-Blanching: easier blanching variety

Crop Notes

Needs consistent moisture and rich soil for crisp stalks.

title: Carrot

frontmatter.Start Method: See planting calendar and crop notes for direct sow vs transplant guidance.

html:
Carrot

Quick Use Guide

  • Seed depth: 1/4-1/2 in
  • Spacing: rows 12-18 in, plants 1-2 in
  • Feeding level: Light
  • Rotation note: Great cleanup crop after heavy feeders

Planting Calendar (Zone 7B)

  • Spring sow/plant window: Mar 15-Apr 15
  • Fall sow/plant window: Jul 15-Aug 1
  • Outdoor transplant window: Direct sow only
  • Indoor start lead time: Direct sow preferred
  • Frost baseline used: last spring frost Apr 5-Apr 15; first fall frost Oct 25-Nov 5.

Seed Depth and Spacing

  • Seed/planting depth: 1/4-1/2 in
  • Row spacing: 12-18 in
  • In-row spacing: 1-2 in
  • Timing windows verified against Virginia Cooperative Extension (Table 3) and Clemson Extension planting chart.

Transplanting Time and Procedure

  • Direct sow/planting is the standard method for this crop.
  • Prepare a fine seedbed and keep topsoil evenly moist through germination.
  • Thin seedlings early to final spacing to reduce disease pressure.

Companion Plants

  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Onion]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Lettuce]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Pea]]
  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Chives]]

Avoid Planting Near

  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Dill]] (especially with carrots when mature)
  • Compacted soil and fresh manure

Varieties

  • Nantes: sweet blunt-tipped roots
  • Danvers 126: reliable storage type
  • Scarlet Nantes: smooth roots for fresh eating

Crop Notes

Keep topsoil evenly moist through germination.

Verified References

  • Virginia Cooperative Extension - Home Vegetable Gardening in Virginia (Table 3)
  • Clemson Cooperative Extension - Planning a Garden (Table 2)
  • WVU Extension - Companion Planting in the Garden
  • University of Maryland Extension - Planting Transplants in Your Garden

Bloom Timing and Pollinator Notes

  • Start method: See planting calendar and crop notes for direct sow vs transplant guidance.
  • Days to germination: 5-14 days
  • Bloom from seed: 50-90 days
  • Pollinator value: Moderate

title: Cantaloupe

frontmatter.Start Method: See planting calendar and crop notes for direct sow vs transplant guidance.
frontmatter.Tags: cantaloupe, zone-7b, vegetable, bloom-fast, pollinator-moderate, pollinator-high

html:
Cantaloupe

Quick Use Guide

  • Seed depth: 1 in
  • Spacing: rows 60-72 in, plants 24-36 in
  • Feeding level: Heavy
  • Rotation note: Do not repeat cucurbits in same bed yearly

Planting Calendar (Zone 7B)

  • Spring sow/plant window: Apr 25-Jun 15
  • Outdoor transplant window: Apr 25-May 20 (optional transplants)
  • Indoor start lead time: Start 2-3 weeks before transplanting
  • Frost baseline used: last spring frost Apr 5-Apr 15; first fall frost Oct 25-Nov 5.

Seed Depth and Spacing

  • Seed/planting depth: 1 in
  • Row spacing: 60-72 in
  • In-row spacing: 24-36 in
  • Timing windows verified against Virginia Cooperative Extension (Table 3) and Clemson Extension planting chart.

Transplanting Time and Procedure

  • Direct sow is effective once soil is warm enough.
  • Optional transplanting can speed harvest by 1-2 weeks.
  • Harden off starts for 7-10 days and avoid root disturbance.
  • Water in deeply after planting and keep moisture steady until new growth.

Companion Plants

  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Bean Bush]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Corn Sweet]]
  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Dill]]
  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Nasturtium]]

Avoid Planting Near

  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Potato]]
  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Fennel]]

Varieties

  • Hales Best: classic netted melon
  • Athena: strong disease resistance
  • Sugar Cube: smaller personal-size fruits

Crop Notes

Use black plastic or mulch to warm soil quickly.

Verified References

  • Virginia Cooperative Extension - Home Vegetable Gardening in Virginia (Table 3)
  • Clemson Cooperative Extension - Planning a Garden (Table 2)
  • WVU Extension - Companion Planting in the Garden
  • University of Maryland Extension - Planting Transplants in Your Garden

Bloom Timing and Pollinator Notes

  • Start method: See planting calendar and crop notes for direct sow vs transplant guidance.
  • Days to germination: 5-14 days
  • Bloom from seed: 50-90 days
  • Pollinator value: Moderate

- Harden off starts for 7-10 days before planting outdoors.
- Transplant in late afternoon or on cloudy days to reduce shock.
- Water in thoroughly and mulch after soil has warmed.

## Companion Plants
- [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Onion]]
- [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Garlic]]
- [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Dill]]
- [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Chamomile]]

## Avoid Planting Near
- [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Tomato]]
- [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Bean Pole]]

## Varieties
- Long Island Improved: heirloom standard
- Diablo: uniform hybrid sprouts
- Jade Cross: compact early hybrid

## Crop Notes
Flavor improves after light frost.

## Verified References

- Bloom from seed: 50-90 days
- Pollinator value: Moderate

title: Beet

frontmatter.Start Method: See planting calendar and crop notes for direct sow vs transplant guidance.

html:
Beet

Quick Use Guide

  • Seed depth: 1/2 in
  • Spacing: rows 12-18 in, plants 2-3 in
  • Feeding level: Light
  • Rotation note: Excellent after heavy feeders

Planting Calendar (Zone 7B)

  • Spring sow/plant window: Mar 15-Apr 15
  • Fall sow/plant window: Jul 15-Aug 15
  • Outdoor transplant window: Direct sow for best roots
  • Indoor start lead time: Direct sow preferred
  • Frost baseline used: last spring frost Apr 5-Apr 15; first fall frost Oct 25-Nov 5.

Seed Depth and Spacing

  • Seed/planting depth: 1/2 in
  • Row spacing: 12-18 in
  • In-row spacing: 2-3 in
  • Timing windows verified against Virginia Cooperative Extension (Table 3) and Clemson Extension planting chart.

Transplanting Time and Procedure

  • Direct sow/planting is the standard method for this crop.
  • Prepare a fine seedbed and keep topsoil evenly moist through germination.
  • Thin seedlings early to final spacing to reduce disease pressure.

Companion Plants

  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Onion]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Lettuce]]
  • [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Pea]]
  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Chives]]

Avoid Planting Near

  • [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Dill]] (especially with carrots when mature)
  • Compacted soil and fresh manure

Varieties

  • Detroit Dark Red: standard storage beet
  • Chioggia: candy-stripe interior
  • Cylindra: long roots for slicing

Crop Notes

Thin clusters early; each seed ball can sprout multiple plants.

Verified References

  • Virginia Cooperative Extension - Home Vegetable Gardening in Virginia (Table 3)
  • Clemson Cooperative Extension - Planning a Garden (Table 2)
  • WVU Extension - Companion Planting in the Garden
  • University of Maryland Extension - Planting Transplants in Your Garden

Bloom Timing and Pollinator Notes

  • Start method: See planting calendar and crop notes for direct sow vs transplant guidance.
  • Days to germination: 5-14 days
  • Bloom from seed: 50-90 days
  • Pollinator value: Moderate

- Outdoor transplant window: Mar 1-Apr 15 (crowns)
- Indoor start lead time: Use crowns for quickest establishment
- Frost baseline used: last spring frost Apr 5-Apr 15; first fall frost Oct 25-Nov 5.

## Seed Depth and Spacing
- Seed/planting depth: 6-8 in (crowns)
- Row spacing: 36-42 in
- In-row spacing: 12-18 in
- Timing windows verified against Virginia Cooperative Extension (Table 3) and Clemson Extension planting chart.

## Transplanting Time and Procedure
- Direct sow/planting is the standard method for this crop.
- Prepare a fine seedbed and keep topsoil evenly moist through germination.
- Thin seedlings early to final spacing to reduce disease pressure.

## Companion Plants
- [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Tomato]]
- [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Parsley]]
- [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Basil]]
- [[Herbalism-Vault/01_Plants/Marigold]]

## Avoid Planting Near
- [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Onion]]
- [[Vegetable-Vault/01_Crops/Garlic]]

## Varieties
- Jersey Knight: all-male high-yield spears
- Mary Washington: classic heirloom with good flavor
- Purple Passion: sweeter purple spears

## Crop Notes
Do not harvest heavily the first two seasons.

## Verified References
- [Virginia Cooperative Extension - Home Vegetable Gardening in Virginia (Table 3)](https://www.pubs.ext.vt.edu/426/426-331/426-331.html)
- [Clemson Cooperative Extension - Planning a Garden (Table 2)](https://hgic.clemson.edu/factsheet/planning-a-garden/)
- [WVU Extension - Companion Planting in the Garden](https://extension.wvu.edu/lawn-gardening-pests/gardening/garden-management/companion-planting)
- [University of Maryland Extension - Planting Transplants in Your Garden](https://extension.umd.edu/resource/planting-transplants-your-garden/)

## Bloom Timing and Pollinator Notes
- Start method: See planting calendar and crop notes for direct sow vs transplant guidance.
- Days to germination: 5-14 days
- Bloom from seed: 50-90 days
- Pollinator value: Moderate

title: Tithonia

frontmatter.Start Method: Start indoors 4-6 weeks before outplanting.

html:
Tithonia

Seed Starting

  • Method: Start indoors 4-6 weeks before outplanting.
  • Start window: Mar-May
  • Days to germination: 7-14 days

Bloom Timing

  • Bloom from seed: 75-95 days
  • Bloom speed tier: Moderate
  • Indoor bloom viability: Limited

Pollinator Notes

  • Pollinator value: High
  • Nectar level: High
  • Pollen level: Moderate
  • Strategy: Strong late spring to summer nectar producer.

Companion Use

  • Pair with herbs and vegetables needing pollinator pressure.
  • Link into [[Master-Indexes/Pollinator Network]] and [[Master-Indexes/Companion Matrix]].
  • For early greenhouse pollinator support, combine with [[Wildflower-Vault/01_Plants/Alyssum]].

id: Master-Indexes/Source References - Seed and Bloom Data.md
path: Master-Indexes/Source References - Seed and Bloom Data.md
title: Source References - Seed and Bloom Data
html:
Source References - Seed Start and Bloom Timing

Primary references used for seed-start and bloom-speed normalization:

  • Virginia Cooperative Extension: https://www.pubs.ext.vt.edu/426/426-331/426-331.html
  • Clemson Cooperative Extension: https://hgic.clemson.edu/factsheet/planning-a-garden/
  • Johnny's Selected Seeds (grower library): https://www.johnnyseeds.com/growers-library/
  • Benary technical guides: https://www.benary.com/
  • University of Maryland Extension transplant guidance: https://extension.umd.edu/resource/planting-transplants-your-garden/

Notes:

  • Bloom days are practical ranges and vary by cultivar, light intensity, nutrition, and temperature.
  • Indoor greenhouse assumptions: 14-16h light, warm root zone, consistent fertility, and minimal transplant shock.
raw:
# Source References - Seed Start and Bloom Timing

Primary references used for seed-start and bloom-speed normalization:

- Virginia Cooperative Extension: https://www.pubs.ext.vt.edu/426/426-331/426-331.html
- Clemson Cooperative Extension: https://hgic.clemson.edu/factsheet/planning-a-garden/
- Johnny's Selected Seeds (grower library): https://www.johnnyseeds.com/growers-library/
- Benary technical guides: https://www.benary.com/
- University of Maryland Extension transplant guidance: https://extension.umd.edu/resource/planting-transplants-your-garden/

Notes:
- Bloom days are practical ranges and vary by cultivar, light intensity, nutrition, and temperature.
- Indoor greenhouse assumptions: 14-16h light, warm root zone, consistent fertility, and minimal transplant shock.
